user_query:
I need you to process my backlog into our local SQLite-backed TODO system and produce a clean snapshot and notes.

Read input/backlog.jsonl and apply every action in order. Use the file’s client_ref values (like "t1") to track which created task maps to which later update. After all actions are applied:
- For every task that is currently status=open and has a non-null remind_at, store a cron_job_id in the task using the exact format "cron_<task_id>".
- Do not set or keep a cron_job_id on tasks that are not open (e.g., done/blocked/expired/forgotten) or tasks with no remind_at.

Then write the following files under output/:
1) output/applied_actions.json — A summary with these keys and exact names:
   - total_actions (int)
   - added_count (int)
   - updated_count (int) [count only set-tags, set-priority, set-status, set-dates]
   - marked_done_count (int)
   - reopened_count (int)
   - deleted_count (int)
   - errors (array)
   - add_index_to_id (object) mapping each client_ref (e.g., "t1") to the created integer task id

2) output/open_tasks_by_priority.json — A JSON object with a key "tasks" whose value is an array of open tasks only, sorted by priority ascending then id ascending. Each task object must include exactly these fields: id, title, priority, status, tags, due_at, remind_at, cron_job_id.

3) output/stale_candidates.json — Run the stale-candidate check using p3_days=30, p2_days=45, and now="2026-06-15T00:00:00+00:00". Save the full JSON result as-is.

4) output/notes.md — A short write-up (bulleted is fine) with these section headings: "Overview", "Reminders Scheduled", and "Stale Review Plan". Explain any notable rationale (e.g., why something is blocked or deleted), and next steps for the remaining open items.

Please make sure the four files are created exactly at the specified paths.
